Covers the Holy Roman Empire through its dissolution in 1806 under Napoleonic pressure including constitutional structures shaping German-speaking Europe, relevant to the smaller German states under French influence.

Covers German literary and intellectual culture from Sturm und Drang through Weimar Classicism and Romanticism to the Franco-Prussian War era, relevant to the Romantic movement and developments in arts, philosophy, and religion.
